tasks.description: Define and document required changes based on analytical conclusions.

subtasks.description: Categorize tickers to keep, adjust, or remove based on all analyses.
completion_evidence: Strong Risk-Adjusted Return, but High Beta; Clustered High Correlations; Several High-Risk, High-Drawdown Outliers. Portfolio Sharpe (2.27) and Sortino (4.52) are excellent and volatility is manageable at 0.24, but Beta (1.04) is above market, with annualized return (73.2%) potentially reflecting momentum-driven, high-risk clustering. Pairwise correlation matrix reveals semi, tech-software, and REIT clusters (e.g., ARM-AVGO-MU-MTSI-NVDA have 0.55-0.65+ intra-cluster correlation). REITs (FR-TRNO-STAG) and Utilities (AEE-WEC-CMS) also display high cross-correlations (>0.7 in some), amplifying exposure risk and hampering diversification. Stress test reveals multiple aggressive/microcap positions (DGNX, OKLO, CRDO, ARM, APP, MU) with catastrophic drawdowns under real and hypothetical scenarios (credit/beta shocks), offsetting strength of defensive positions. High-beta tickers include DGNX, CRDO, OKLO, ARM, MU (all show negative or huge drawdown risk in stress scenarios), with some like DGNX and CRDO producing positive returns only in idiosyncratic or momentum rallies (high tail risk). Defensive/liquidity anchors like IGSB, VCSH, and select Utilities dampen risk but also correlate strongly with each other. Tickers to Remove: DGNX, OKLO, CRDO, STAG, FR (due to catastrophic or chronic stress fail), and at least one low-liquidity, high-drawdown semi (ARM or MTSI). Tickers to Reduce: AVGO (overlapping beta/correlation), AEE/WEC/CMS (keep only top risk-reward, reduce cluster), and NVDA (remains leader, but size harmonized for risk budget). Tickers to Keep/Core: NVDA, PLTR, BSX, IGSB, VCSH, NRG, EQIX, GREK, EMQQ, NLR, SLVR, JPM, TRNO, APP, SGDM, FUFU (monitor for improvement or replacement), and CEG. Several ETF and financials allocations can be modestly increased to improve pattern. Semi cluster will be rebuilt with new, low-correlation growth names. Action needed: add new lower-beta, noncorrelated tickers to replace risk cluster exits, and tune core sector allocations for drawdown resilience. This will reduce beta/correlation and improve loss scenario outcomes without sacrificing alpha.

observations: Tool 'add_task_evidence' returned: True

subtasks.description: Assemble the revised portfolio with new allocations and add rationale in thesis for each holding.
Store the new optimized portfolio and thesis to episodic_remember.

tasks.description: Run full analytics on the optimized portfolio for validation and fine-tuning.

subtasks.description: Run analytics: calculate_portfolio_performance, calculate_portfolio_returns_metrics, calculate_ticker_performances, calculate_portfolio_correlation_matrix, calculate_portfolio_beta_vs_index, portfolio_exposure_calculator, portfolio_stress_test, and calculate_portfolio_factor_tilts.

tasks.description: Iteratively adjust portfolio and validate improvements until all user preferences are met.

subtasks.description: Make incremental changes and rerun core analytics until portfolio achieves desired beta, correlation, risk-adjusted returns, and alpha potential.

tasks.description: Format and output final optimized portfolio in strict JSON, documenting additions, removals, and adjustments.

subtasks.description: Format output according to specification with full portfolio allocations and thesis; include changes section.

execution_history.evidence: Strong Risk-Adjusted Return, but High Beta; Clustered High Correlations; Several High-Risk, High-Drawdown Outliers. Portfolio Sharpe (2.27) and Sortino (4.52) are excellent and volatility is manageable at 0.24, but Beta (1.04) is above market, with annualized return (73.2%) potentially reflecting momentum-driven, high-risk clustering. Pairwise correlation matrix reveals semi, tech-software, and REIT clusters (e.g., ARM-AVGO-MU-MTSI-NVDA have 0.55-0.65+ intra-cluster correlation). REITs (FR-TRNO-STAG) and Utilities (AEE-WEC-CMS) also display high cross-correlations (>0.7 in some), amplifying exposure risk and hampering diversification. Stress test reveals multiple aggressive/microcap positions (DGNX, OKLO, CRDO, ARM, APP, MU) with catastrophic drawdowns under real and hypothetical scenarios (credit/beta shocks), offsetting strength of defensive positions. High-beta tickers include DGNX, CRDO, OKLO, ARM, MU (all show negative or huge drawdown risk in stress scenarios), with some like DGNX and CRDO producing positive returns only in idiosyncratic or momentum rallies (high tail risk). Defensive/liquidity anchors like IGSB, VCSH, and select Utilities dampen risk but also correlate strongly with each other. Tickers to Remove: DGNX, OKLO, CRDO, STAG, FR (due to catastrophic or chronic stress fail), and at least one low-liquidity, high-drawdown semi (ARM or MTSI). Tickers to Reduce: AVGO (overlapping beta/correlation), AEE/WEC/CMS (keep only top risk-reward, reduce cluster), and NVDA (remains leader, but size harmonized for risk budget). Tickers to Keep/Core: NVDA, PLTR, BSX, IGSB, VCSH, NRG, EQIX, GREK, EMQQ, NLR, SLVR, JPM, TRNO, APP, SGDM, FUFU (monitor for improvement or replacement), and CEG. Several ETF and financials allocations can be modestly increased to improve pattern. Semi cluster will be rebuilt with new, low-correlation growth names. Action needed: add new lower-beta, noncorrelated tickers to replace risk cluster exits, and tune core sector allocations for drawdown resilience. This will reduce beta/correlation and improve loss scenario outcomes without sacrificing alpha.

execution_history.reason: Tickers categorized based on performance, risk/beta, and stress tests: Remove (DGNX, OKLO, CRDO, STAG, FR, one of ARM/MTSI), Reduce (AVGO, cluster utilities, NVDA), Keep/Core (NVDA, PLTR, BSX, IGSB, VCSH, NRG, EQIX, GREK, EMQQ, NLR, SLVR, JPM, TRNO, APP, SGDM, FUFU, CEG). Portfolio requires new additions for diversification, replacing high-beta and high-correlation names to optimize risk-adjusted returns.
